tier1.job_description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
The Director of Business Affairs will play a pivotal role in shaping the negotiation strategy and managing the business relationships for the audio Entertainment Originals portfolio. In this role you will collaborate with creative executives and top tier partners in audio, TV, film, music and theater as they work to deliver Audible customers high-quality original content for adaptation on the air, screen and stage. You will report into to the Head of Business Affairs, Entertainment Originals.

ABOUT YOU
You are a valued strategic partner with a history of structuring and closing deals in the entertainment and media industries and are ready to assist the global Audible team in negotiating terms that benefit Audible and delights its customers.

As a Director of Business Affairs, you will...
- Serve as lead negotiator on deals for creators, producers and rights-holders of original audio content for the development, acquisition, and production of entertainment originals
- Evaluate, structure, negotiate, and implement a wide range of agreements independently, while partnering with legal to finalize the full long-form agreements
- Define strategies, expectations, and priorities to ensure team actions align with company success
- Partner with leadership and legal teams to identify and address upfront possible business and legal risks associated with proposed productions, while championing best practices
- Collaborate with internal teams and leadership on the development of strategies for new content initiatives
- Manage, prioritize, and execute high volume deal flow against specific metrics
- Track and communicate deal terms within the organization and provide for first line of enforcement of deal terms, if necessary
- Grow and maintain strong relationships with existing and potential entertainment industry partners such as film/TV studios, celebrities, production studios, writers, agents, managers, and publishers
- Build relationships and represent the entertainment deals’ team to various internal stakeholders including Editorial, Creative, Finance, BI, Marketing, PR, Content Partnerships, and Content Operations teams.
- Work cross-functionally across Audible and other Amazon affiliates (e.g., Amazon Music, Amazon Publishing, etc.) to execute against complex deals and partnership opportunities
- Identify and prepare projects for internal support and valuation by presenting data, preparing budgets, P&Ls, and drafting proposed deal terms - Passionately work on the evolution and consumption of audio content and storytelling

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.

At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives.
It's why we work with some of the world's leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners.
We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other.
Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
We're seeking a Head of Production, North America to lead Audible's in-house audiovisual production teams across the U.
S.
and Canada, including for audiobooks, audio dramas, podcasts, video content, and live production.
Reporting to the Head of Audible Content, North America, you will execute and oversee all types of production from end-to-end.
As the Head of Production, North America, you will work closely with the Heads of Creative Development and Production Management & Operations to establish and elevate the quality bar for all Audible-produced content, including Audible Originals and Audible Books.
You have a strong creative acumen, financial stewardship, senior management experience, and the ability to implement processes efficiently and sustainably without sacrificing creative excellence or intent.
You balance creative ambition, operational rigor, and strong leadership thriving at the intersection of storytelling, business strategy, and scalable systems.

As the Head of Production, North America, you will.
• Lead production execution, financial oversight, and strategic planning for the studio and production teams
• Foster a culture rooted in collaboration, creative excellence, inclusivity, and efficiency
• Serve as Audible's senior production executive to internal stakeholders and the external creative community
• Lead senior producers, sound designers, directors, and video producers across North America to deliver a high-volume, high-quality slate of Audible Originals, Audible Books, podcasts, and other content types that meet Audible's audience, creative, and business objectives
• Work closely with the Head of Production & Operations to oversee the overall studio budget, P&L, and vendor strategy, optimizing for creative quality, fiscal accountability, and scalability
• Inspire the team to embrace new ways to tell stories in audio-first formats and new technologies, including AI-driven tools
• Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external creators and production partners across entertainment, publishing, and podcasting

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling.
We spark listeners' imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives.
We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit.
We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors.
This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.

Qualifications: You have a strong creative acumen, financial stewardship, senior management experience, and the ability to implement processes efficiently and sustainably without sacrificing creative excellence or intent
You balance creative ambition, operational rigor, and strong leadership thriving at the intersection of storytelling, business strategy, and scalable systems
Responsibilities: and Canada, including for audiobooks, audio dramas, podcasts, video content, and live production
Reporting to the Head of Audible Content, North America, you will execute and oversee all types of production from end-to-end

As the Head of Production, North America, you will work closely with the Heads of Creative Development and Production Management & Operations to establish and elevate the quality bar for all Audible-produced content, including Audible Originals and Audible Books
As the Head of Production, North America, you will
Lead production execution, financial oversight, and strategic planning for the studio and production teams
Foster a culture rooted in collaboration, creative excellence, inclusivity, and efficiency
Serve as Audible's senior production executive to internal stakeholders and the external creative community

Lead senior producers, sound designers, directors, and video producers across North America to deliver a high-volume, high-quality slate of Audible Originals, Audible Books, podcasts, and other content types that meet Audible's audience, creative, and business objectives
Work closely with the Head of Production & Operations to oversee the overall studio budget, P&L, and vendor strategy, optimizing for creative quality, fiscal accountability, and scalability
Inspire the team to embrace new ways to tell stories in audio-first formats and new technologies, including AI-driven tools
Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external creators and production partners across entertainment, publishing, and podcasting

tier1.job_description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
The Associate Director, Production and Operations will play a critical role in building and optimizing the production systems that enable Audible Studios to deliver premium content. This position serves as a strategic operational leader who orchestrates seamless collaboration between cross functional teams and workflows to ensure excellence at every stage of the content lifecycle—from conception to delivery.
This role is ideal for a systems thinker and problem-solver who thrives in dynamic and creative environments, balances quality with efficiency, and builds scalable frameworks that empower creative teams to do their best work.

As an Associate Director of Production and Operations, you will…
- Work closely with creative teams to understand audio content production processes and identify opportunities for improvement
- Facilitate clear communication and seamless handoffs across teams to support end-to-end production from concept through final delivery
- Coordinate recurring and ad hoc cross-team meetings that require multi-departmental involvement
- Identify process inefficiencies and develop solutions that improve both quality and efficiency
- Develop standardized production templates, schedules, and documentation frameworks that enable teams to deliver exceptional content
- Systematically capture lessons learned and feed insights back into workflow design to support continuous improvement
- Design and implement scalable production systems and workflows that support current operations and future innovations
- Implement tools and technologies that enhance operational effectiveness
- Train internal teams and stakeholders on system usage and drive adoption of best practices
- Coach and mentor team members to build operational capabilities
- Lead the integration of new tools, processes, and workflows while supporting teams through change
- Partner with leadership and cross-functional teams to communicate changes, address concerns, and build organizational capability

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.

tier2.job_description:
About Kinetic Brands

Unconventional brands for unconventional people.

Kinetic is a full-stack brand incubator owning a portfolio of digital brands. The brands we build are the opposite of basic. Each brand has a distinct identity, memorable product, and cult following. Through our full-stack infrastructure (meaning we manage everything in-house–including fulfillment, supply chain, R&D, marketing, and development), we can go first to market with any brand concept and create something truly bespoke that stands out even in the most crowded of industries.

About the Role

At Kinetic Brands, we are passionate about creating exceptional products and experiences that resonate with our community. We are seeking a talented Content Producer to join our in-house creative team and own the execution of monthly product launch deliverables. This role is hybrid, with weekly photoshoots and creative collaboration in our New Jersey office.

In this role, you will lead the execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing, owning video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns while ensuring all assets meet a high standard of quality and brand consistency. You will contribute to creative concepting for launches and paid media, translating strategic direction into effective execution in partnership with the Creative and Art Director, while also guiding content producers and freelancers through feedback and QA. You’ll support photoshoots from planning through on-set execution, including second shooting, and drive performance marketing creative through ongoing testing and iteration. With a strong focus on efficiency, you will continuously improve workflows, file organization, and production processes to elevate both output and team performance.

If you’re ambitious, detail-oriented, and excited to contribute to a fast-moving creative team within an established and passionate community, we’d love to hear from you.

You will
Lead execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing Own video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns and ads Oversee and elevate retouching, design, and final asset quality Concept and contribute creative ideas for launches, ads, and campaigns Partner closely with Creative/Art Director to translate strategy into execution Guide content producers and freelancers (feedback, direction, QA) Support photoshoots with planning + second shooting (photo/video) Drive performance marketing creative (Meta ads, testing variations, iteration) Improve workflows, file organization, and production efficiency

You have
BA or equivalent in relevant field 5+ years of relevant experience in content creation, creative production, or digital marketing Experience working in an e-commerce or direct-to-consumer environment A strong portfolio demonstrating high-quality visual execution, with an emphasis on video, storytelling, and brand consistency Excellent time management and prioritization skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work cross-functionally with creative and marketing teams Experience leading or guiding creative work, including providing feedback and direction to team members or freelancers Hands-on experience with Shopify, including product listing management and merchandising Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and Google Workspace, with strong video editing capabilities Experience developing content for performance marketing channels (Meta, TikTok, paid social) Experience supporting or leading photoshoots, including photography and videography (second shooting or capture experience) Exceptional attention to detail and a strong eye for visual quality and brand execution A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to take ownership, give direction, and continuously improve

Bonus points
Strong awareness of internet culture, social media trends, and digital communities UX/UI experience (desktop and mobile)

Perks
Healthcare You Can Count On – Competitive medical, dental, and vision plans to keep you covered when it matters most. Unlimited PTO – Take the time you need—we trust you (plus, we require a minimum!). Paid Parental Leave – 12 weeks paid + 4 weeks eased return to work. Family first! 401k Match – We match 4% to help grow your future. Monthly Home Office Stipend – We cover part of your phone or internet bill. Employee Discounts – Big savings on our products, just for you!

Work environment
Hybrid: This role requires on-site work at our Passaic, NJ office 2-3 days a week.

Kinetic Brands is an equal opportunity employer — we believe being successful is directly tied to creating a collaborative, diverse, and inclusive environment.

About Kinetic Brands

Unconventional brands for unconventional people.

Kinetic is a full-stack brand incubator owning a portfolio of digital brands. The brands we build are the opposite of basic. Each brand has a distinct identity, memorable product, and cult following. Through our full-stack infrastructure (meaning we manage everything in-house–including fulfillment, supply chain, R&D, marketing, and development), we can go first to market with any brand concept and create something truly bespoke that stands out even in the most crowded of industries.

About the Role

At Kinetic Brands, we are passionate about creating exceptional products and experiences that resonate with our community. We are seeking a talented Content Producer to join our in-house creative team and own the execution of monthly product launch deliverables. This role is hybrid, with weekly photoshoots and creative collaboration in our New Jersey office.

In this role, you will lead the execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing, owning video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns while ensuring all assets meet a high standard of quality and brand consistency. You will contribute to creative concepting for launches and paid media, translating strategic direction into effective execution in partnership with the Creative and Art Director, while also guiding content producers and freelancers through feedback and QA. You’ll support photoshoots from planning through on-set execution, including second shooting, and drive performance marketing creative through ongoing testing and iteration. With a strong focus on efficiency, you will continuously improve workflows, file organization, and production processes to elevate both output and team performance.

If you’re ambitious, detail-oriented, and excited to contribute to a fast-moving creative team within an established and passionate community, we’d love to hear from you.

You will
• Lead execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing
• Own video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns and ads
• Oversee and elevate retouching, design, and final asset quality
• Concept and contribute creative ideas for launches, ads, and campaigns
• Partner closely with Creative/Art Director to translate strategy into execution
• Guide content producers and freelancers (feedback, direction, QA)
• Support photoshoots with planning + second shooting (photo/video)
• Drive performance marketing creative (Meta ads, testing variations, iteration)
• Improve workflows, file organization, and production efficiency

You have
• BA or equivalent in relevant field
• 5+ years of relevant experience in content creation, creative production, or digital marketing
• Experience working in an e-commerce or direct-to-consumer environment
• A strong portfolio demonstrating high-quality visual execution, with an emphasis on video, storytelling, and brand consistency
• Excellent time management and prioritization skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
• Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work cross-functionally with creative and marketing teams
• Experience leading or guiding creative work, including providing feedback and direction to team members or freelancers
• Hands-on experience with Shopify, including product listing management and merchandising
• Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and Google Workspace, with strong video editing capabilities
• Experience developing content for performance marketing channels (Meta, TikTok, paid social)
• Experience supporting or leading photoshoots, including photography and videography (second shooting or capture experience)
• Exceptional attention to detail and a strong eye for visual quality and brand execution
• A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to take ownership, give direction, and continuously improve

Bonus points
• Strong awareness of internet culture, social media trends, and digital communities
• UX/UI experience (desktop and mobile)

Perks
• Healthcare You Can Count On – Competitive medical, dental, and vision plans to keep you covered when it matters most.
• Unlimited PTO – Take the time you need—we trust you (plus, we require a minimum!).
• Paid Parental Leave – 12 weeks paid + 4 weeks eased return to work. Family first!
• 401k Match – We match 4% to help grow your future.
• Monthly Home Office Stipend – We cover part of your phone or internet bill.
• Employee Discounts – Big savings on our products, just for you!

Work environment
• Hybrid: This role requires on-site work at our Passaic, NJ office 2-3 days a week.

Kinetic Brands is an equal opportunity employer — we believe being successful is directly tied to creating a collaborative, diverse, and inclusive environment.

A company is looking for a Senior Producer, Live Content. Key Responsibilities Own production delivery for live content initiatives including seasonal content and collaborations Drive execution from concept through release validation in partnership with various stakeholders Identify production risks early and lead mitigation strategies across cross-functional teams Required Qualifications 5+ years of hands-on production experience in game development or live service environments Demonstrated ownership of delivery for complex content releases or multi-team initiatives Experience shipping updates on a live game with active player impact Proven experience working with agile production workflows and iterative development environments Advanced experience with Jira and Confluence as production infrastructure

Searching for cast and crew members for "Maybe We Were Fire." a romantic comedy-drama, feature film set in New York, Long Island.

Logline: Danny and Eve first met when they were 18 and fell madly in love. They married young at the end of their senior year of high school and said “I do” fast. Now in their mid-twenties, the spark they once had – their “fire” has run out and they are divorcing. The once inseparable pair finds themselves at a crossroads as they both must navigate what it means to move on from the person you loved the most.

Responsibilities: Logline: Danny and Eve first met when they were 18 and fell madly in love
They married young at the end of their senior year of high school and said “I do” fast
